title: PHP echo and print
content:
### PHP echo and print

`echo` and `print` are language constructs for outputting text.

### echo

`echo` can output one or more strings separated by commas. It has no return value.

```php
<?php
echo "Hello World!";
echo "This ", "is ", "multiple ", "params";
echo "Line 1\nLine 2";
?>
```

### print

`print` outputs a single string and always returns `1`.

```php
<?php
$result = print "Hello";  // prints Hello
// $result is 1
?>
```

### Comparison

| Feature | echo | print |
|---------|------|-------|
| Return value | None | 1 |
| Multiple params | Yes | No |
| Speed | Slightly faster | Slower |
| Parentheses | Optional | Optional |
| Used with expressions | No | Yes (returns value) |

> **Key Point:** `echo` is the most commonly used output construct in PHP because it is faster and supports multiple parameters.
codeExample:
Learning $name"; // print example if (print "
Success") { echo "
Print returned 1"; } ?>

quiz.question: Can echo take multiple string parameters?
